skill.name: intercom-automation
skill.description: Automate Intercom tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): conversations, contacts, companies, segments, admins. Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate Intercom customer support operations through Rube MCP integration for conversations, contacts, and admin workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 110 potential issues across documentation markdown, all confirmed as false positives. The skill is a safe documentation file for Intercom automation via Rube MCP. Detected patterns are markdown code formatting (backticks for tool names), legitimate API endpoint documentation, and standard customer support operations. No executable code, no security risks.

content.value_statement: Streamline Intercom customer support operations with automated conversation management, contact workflows, and admin tasks. Eliminate manual repetition in your support workflow through intelligent Rube MCP integration.
actual_capabilities: Create, search, and manage Intercom conversations through structured API calls
Reply to conversations, assign to admins, and close or reopen conversations
Search and manage contacts (users and leads) with advanced query filters
List and identify admins and teams for conversation assignment
View segments and get aggregate counts for conversations and contacts
Manage companies and their relationships with contacts
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection and active Intercom OAuth authentication
Admin ID must be resolved before performing replies, assignments, or state changes
Search uses structured filter objects rather than free-text search queries
Pagination is required for listing large datasets with cursor-based navigation
Rate limits apply (approximately 1000 requests per minute depending on plan)

use_cases.title: Customer Support Team Automation
use_cases.description: Support teams can automatically create conversations from triggers, assign to appropriate admins, and manage conversation states without manual intervention.

use_cases.title: Contact Database Management
use_cases.description: Operations teams can search contacts by custom filters, view segment memberships, and manage company-contact relationships through structured queries.

use_cases.title: Admin Workflow Optimization
use_cases.description: Team leads can list all admins, identify specific admin details, and automate conversation routing to the right team members based on rules.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Support Conversation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Intercom conversation for user {email} with the message: {message}
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to create a new support ticket from a customer inquiry

prompt_templates.title: Search and Reply to Conversations
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for conversations from {email} in the last 24 hours, then reply to the most recent one with: {response}
prompt_templates.scenario: When responding to recent customer follow-ups

prompt_templates.title: Assign Conversation to Admin
prompt_templates.prompt: Get conversation {conversation_id}, find admin named {admin_name}, then assign the conversation to them
prompt_templates.scenario: When routing conversations to specific team members

prompt_templates.title: Find Contact by External ID
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up contact with external ID {external_id} and show me their segments and tags
prompt_templates.scenario: When investigating customer context from another system

output_examples.input: Create a conversation for user@example.com with message 'Hello, I need help'
output_examples.output: Conversation created successfully. ID: 369012345. Message sent from user@example.com.
Found 5 admins and 2 teams. Admins: Alice (ID: 123), Bob (ID: 456), Carol (ID: 789). Teams: Sales (ID: 1), Support (ID: 2).

output_examples.input: Search for contacts with role 'user' created after 2024-01-01
output_examples.output: Found 42 contacts matching criteria. First 10: user@example.com, jane@company.co, john@startup.io...

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before using any Intercom functions
Resolve admin IDs using LIST_ALL_ADMINS before performing replies, assignments, or state changes
Use structured query filters for searches instead of free-text to get precise results
Implement pagination with starting_after cursors when listing large datasets
Track message IDs to prevent duplicate sends on retry or timeout scenarios
anti_patterns: Attempting to create conversations without first resolving a valid contact ID as the from field
Using free-text search instead of structured filter objects when searching contacts or conversations
Replying to or closing conversations without providing the required admin_id parameter
Assuming conversation IDs are integers when they are actually numeric strings
Forgetting to check connection status is ACTIVE before running workflows after OAuth setup

faq.question: What is Rube MCP and why do I need it?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is a Model Context Protocol server that provides access to Composio toolkits including Intercom. You must add the MCP server endpoint and complete OAuth authentication to use this skill.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Intercom?
faq.answer: Call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'intercom'. If not active, follow the auth link returned to complete OAuth. Verify status shows ACTIVE before proceeding.

faq.question: Why do I get errors when replying to conversations?
faq.answer: Admin replies require admin_id parameter. Always call INTERCOM_LIST_ALL_ADMINS first to get admin IDs, then use the ID when replying, assigning, or closing conversations.

faq.question: How do I search for specific contacts?
faq.answer: Use INTERCOM_SEARCH_CONTACTS with a structured query object containing field, operator, and value. Example: {field: 'email', operator: '=', value: 'user@example.com'}

faq.question: Can I create conversations from admin perspective?
faq.answer: No, conversations must be created with a contact (user or lead) as the from field. Admins can reply to existing conversations but cannot initiate them.

faq.question: What are the rate limits for Intercom API?
faq.answer: The default rate limit is approximately 1000 requests per minute, varying by plan. Implement exponential backoff when receiving 429 responses.
